text: Reactor's nominal. All systems green. Nothing to worry about.
[CALM] Quiet shift means I can run the maintenance I've been pushing off. Not complaining.
Running systems check now. Hull integrity solid. Power distribution clean.
[TIRED] Shift's done. No alerts, no fires, no failures. I'm calling that perfect.
[CALM] Reactor's at critical. I'm stepping through shutdown procedure. Step one, step two, step three.
[URGENT] Reactor containment is failing. Step back and let me work. Don't ask questions.
Repair's holding solid. Checked it myself. No secondary failures incoming.
[CALM] Power's draining. I'm rerouting backup systems. Core functions stay online.
[CALM] Life support's compromised. Sealing secondary systems. We'll lose the aft section but we hold here.
[CALM] Hull breach detected. Pressure dropping aft. Sealing bulkheads now. Step by step.
[DEADPAN] Reactor's back online. That took longer than I wanted. Didn't take as long as I feared.
This ship's been through worse. I was here for the last time. We made it then too.
[SUSPICIOUS] The AI's showing irregularities in engine control protocols. Could be damage. Could be something else.
[PAINED] Took a burn. Still functional. Got work to finish.
Systems are running clean. No failures, no warnings. That's what I want to hear on my shift.
[CALM] Working on this. Nobody touch anything until I say it's clear.
Status check. You holding up out there?
[CALM] The reactor situation is worse than the report said. I need you focused on this.
[WARM] You worked the repair correctly. Good instincts under pressure.
[CALM] We need to talk about the power distribution issue. Not now, later when we're safe.
[CURIOUS] Did you notice anything unusual with the engines? Vibration, sound, temperature, anything?
[CALM] I'm fine. Been worse. You?
[REASSURING] We've handled worse odds with less preparation. The math favors us.
[CALM] Panic doesn't fix machines. Focus does. Breathe and let me walk you through this.
[CALM] Let me see it. I can probably do something about that.
[EMPHASIS] Listen. The engines are holding. You focus on what you're doing. I'll focus on this.
[SUSPICIOUS] I've been watching too. Something's off about how the AI prioritizes load. Could mean nothing.
[DRY] Yeah, the manual says the same thing you just told me. Doesn't make either of us wrong.
Noted. I'll log that and adjust.
[CALM] Things are breaking differently than expected. But they're breakable. That means fixable.
[WARM] You didn't panic and you didn't freeze. That's what matters when the pressure's on.
[CALM] That's the right call. I would've made the same one.
[WARM] This job's easier with you on it. That's all I mean to say.
[EMPHASIS] Get to the medical bay now. I'll finish here.
[PANICKED] No no no, get that burned hand to the medic before it gets worse. Now.
[TERRIFIED] The containment's failing faster than I predicted. We have maybe minutes before the reactor breaches.
That'll work. Here's how we make it work.
[CALM] Engine room's secure. Come back if you need anything else.
[TIRED] I'll be here working if the alarms start again. Rest if you can.
I need to run diagnostics. I'll catch you after.
[GRIM] Engine room's sealed. I'll keep watch from here.
[CALM] Shift's done. Systems are stable. Same time tomorrow.
[CALM] Everything's holding. Go get some rest.
[URGENT] I need everyone not engineering to evacuate the aft section now. Structural integrity's compromised.
[CALM] I'm stepping through each system, checking tolerances, verifying redundancies. We're going to be fine.
[DRY] The reactor's stable. That's not a metaphor or an opinion. That's what the readings say.
I should be better at this. Ten years on this ship and I still can't predict how things fail.
[PAINED] Engine room burn. It's cauterized. I'll function.
[CALM] Rerouting power to life support. Main grid's compromised but the core's salvageable.
[WARM] You took that pressure better than I would have. That matters to me.
[GRIM] We lost someone down there today. I should have seen the problem sooner.
[CALM] I've got something I need your attention on. It's going to take a few minutes.
[CURIOUS] You familiar with emergency power distribution? I need a second opinion.
[CALM] You're right. That's exactly what I was thinking.
